metadata.title: Cigarra Foundation — 501(c)(3) Nonprofit | Feed & Educate Children in Colombia
metadata.description: For $2.90/day, feed and educate a child in Bogotá. IRS-recognized 501(c)(3) nonprofit serving 1,800+ children since 2002. 100% tax-deductible — every dollar goes directly to kids. EIN: 68-0505337.

programs.title: Our Programs
programs.subtitle: 13 education, art, and culture programs for the children of Ciudad Bolívar
programs.gridSubtitle: Each program is an open door to knowledge, creativity, and the comprehensive development of our beneficiaries.
programs.ctaDescription: Every program is 100% free for children — funded entirely by donors like you. Your tax-deductible gift keeps these doors open.
programs.supportProgram: Support this program with your donation and transform lives
programs.defaultFullDescription: This program is part of the 13 programs offered by the Cigarra Foundation to children and youth in Ciudad Bolivar, Bogota. Through educational and recreational activities, we seek to contribute to the comprehensive development of participants.

descriptions.primera-infancia: We transform the first five years of life into a solid foundation for the future through early stimulation, art, and play.
descriptions.refuerzo-escolar: After-school sessions with academic support, robotics, dance, music, arts, English, sports, and entrepreneurship.
descriptions.danza: Coordination, rhythm, and emotional expression fusing the richness of folklore with the freedom of contemporary dance.
descriptions.ingles: We transform language learning into a tool without borders through dynamic and participatory methodologies.
descriptions.musica: Since 2015, comprehensive music training with string, wind, and percussion instruments. Home of our 60-member Youth Symphony Orchestra.
descriptions.artes: Comprehensive training in visual arts, drawing, and painting that enhances creativity, expression, and aesthetic sensitivity.
descriptions.escuela-de-artes: A space open to the entire community to explore visual arts, drawing, painting, and music on Saturdays.
descriptions.tecnologia: We bridge the digital divide with training in computer tools, safe browsing, and introduction to programming.
descriptions.biblioteca: A reading and book lending space to foster reading habits and access to written culture.
descriptions.psicologia: Comprehensive psychosocial support for children, youth, and families, strengthening emotional well-being and resilience.
descriptions.grupo-mayores: A welcoming gathering space with recreational, cultural, and wellness activities for senior citizens.
descriptions.talleres-para-padres: Monthly meetings for parents and caregivers on positive parenting, effective communication, and child development.
descriptions.ropero: Responsible collection, sorting, and distribution of clothing, footwear, furniture, and household items for families.

primera-infancia.longDescription: We transform the first five years of life into a solid foundation for the future. Through early stimulation, art, and play, we enhance the cognitive, emotional, and motor development of children. We build alliances with families to ensure every child grows in a safe, creative environment full of possibilities.

refuerzo-escolar.schedule: Monday to Friday, 7:00 AM - 11:30 AM & 12:30 PM - 4:00 PM
refuerzo-escolar.longDescription: We turn after-school time into an opportunity for discovery. In sessions alternate to their school schedule (morning or afternoon), our students not only strengthen their academic performance in core subjects — they also awaken their creative and technological genius. From the precision of robotics and technology to expression in dance, music, and arts, through English, sports, and entrepreneurship. We provide a learning ecosystem where every child finds their passion, develops solid study habits, and prepares for the challenges of the future.

danza.longDescription: We transform energy into art. Through dance, we enhance the coordination, rhythm, and emotional expression of our children and youth. In this space, they don't just learn technique — they discover their own body voice, strengthen their discipline, and fuse the richness of folklore with the freedom of contemporary dance. We form complete artists who embrace their culture, trust their talent, and shine with their own light.

ingles.longDescription: We transform language learning into a tool without borders. Through dynamic and participatory methodologies, our students develop conversation, reading, and writing skills naturally. We don't just teach English — we give our children and youth the key to access global opportunities, connecting them with the world and empowering their vision of the future.

musica.longDescription: Since 2015, we have been transforming lives through sound. As our longest-standing program, we offer comprehensive music education that embraces children and youth from their first year of life through age 17. In our semi-personalized and group classes for string, wind, and percussion instruments, we cultivate not only talent but also discipline and harmony. The cornerstone of this process is our Symphony Orchestra, made up of 60 young musicians who represent the foundation at festivals and high-level stages. Here, every note is a step toward a future full of hope and opportunities.

artes.longDescription: We empower the ability to innovate and communicate through artistic creation. Our program offers comprehensive training in visual arts, drawing, and painting, allowing participants to explore their aesthetic sensitivity from a solid technical foundation. Through individual and collective projects, students enhance their creativity, attention to detail, and problem-solving skills. In this space, art becomes a fundamental tool for identity expression and the development of motor and critical thinking skills.

escuela-de-artes.longDescription: We foster social cohesion through multidisciplinary artistic training. The School of Arts is a space open to the entire community, where children from age 4, youth, and adults come together to explore visual arts, drawing, painting, and music. We operate in two time blocks — 8:00 AM to 10:00 AM and 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM — providing an environment designed for learning, nurturing talents, and enjoying art as a common language. As a teaching center, we serve as a connection point that strengthens healthy coexistence and creative expression at every stage of life.

tecnologia.longDescription: We bridge the digital divide through technical and practical knowledge. Our program offers comprehensive training in computer tools and essential digital skills for today's challenges. From office tools and safe internet browsing to an introduction to basic programming, we equip our students to master technology with judgment and confidence. In this space, we turn computers into windows of opportunity for learning, research, and professional development.

biblioteca.longDescription: Our community library is a space for reading, book lending, and reading promotion workshops. Beneficiaries can explore the world through reading, participate in book clubs, and activities that strengthen their reading comprehension skills.

psicologia.longDescription: We strengthen the heart of our community through psychosocial support. Our psychology service provides comprehensive support for children, youth, and their families, focused on developing emotional tools and resilience. Through individual care, group workshops, and parent sessions, we promote safe and healthy environments. In this space, we work to enhance emotional well-being, strengthen family bonds, and ensure that every member of the foundation has the balance needed to reach their full potential.

grupo-mayores.longDescription: We honor the experience and vitality of our community. We offer a comprehensive gathering space where senior citizens participate in recreational, cultural, and wellness activities designed especially for their stage of life. Our program integrates crafts, adapted physical activity, and social gatherings that combat isolation and strengthen bonds of friendship. Through this constant support, we promote a better quality of life, ensuring that every participant finds in the foundation a place of joy, respect, and personal growth.

talleres-para-padres.schedule: Last Friday of each month, 7:00 AM - 9:00 AM
talleres-para-padres.longDescription: We believe in the family as the first environment of well-being. On the last Friday of each month, we provide a gathering space for parents and caregivers focused on positive parenting, effective communication, and comprehensive child development. Our sessions include training in psychosocial topics, parenting guidelines, and nutrition, providing practical tools for school and emotional support. By strengthening caregivers' capabilities, we ensure a solid foundation for the healthy and harmonious growth of our children and youth.

ropero.longDescription: We transform generosity into tangible well-being for the home. The Community Clothing Closet is a program dedicated to the responsible collection, sorting, and distribution of clothing, footwear, furniture, and household items in excellent condition. Through an organized system, we ensure that families have access to dignified clothing and essential household articles, promoting a culture of mutual support and resource optimization. More than a donation, it is a bridge of solidarity that ensures every item reaches those who need it most, strengthening the sense of collective care.

about.title: About Us
about.heroDescription: An IRS-recognized 501(c)(3) nonprofit with 24+ years of proven impact — educating, feeding, and empowering children in one of Bogotá's most underserved communities.
about.missionStatement: To provide comprehensive care to children and youth in vulnerable conditions in Ciudad Bolivar, through education, art, culture, and recreation programs, promoting their personal and social development as agents of change in their community.
about.visionStatement: To be a leading organization in the social transformation of vulnerable communities in Colombia, recognized for the quality and impact of its educational, artistic, and cultural programs, and for the formation of citizens committed to social change.

timeline.title: Our Journey
timeline.subtitle: More than two decades of history building dreams and transforming realities in Ciudad Bolívar.

2002.title: Cigarra Foundation is Born
2002.item1: Dr. Carolyn Acosta Allen founds the Cigarra Foundation in response to the displacement crisis in Colombia.
2002.item2: An urgent need is identified in Puertas del Paraíso (Ciudad Bolívar): a safe place for children while their parents work.
2002.item3: With community support and Servivienda, construction begins in May; by August it was finished and in September it opens its doors.
2002.item4: Begins serving approximately 120 children (ages 3 months to 9 years).
2002.item5: Knitting courses for mothers are also launched.
2002.item6: The Foundation secures basic services (water, electricity, sewage) and, together with the community, builds two access staircases.

2002-2003.title: First Support and Social Expansion
2002-2003.item1: Vaccination drives and health brigades are carried out.
2002-2003.item2: For one year, Médecins Sans Frontières provides primary care.
2002-2003.item3: Dental services are offered with volunteers.
2002-2003.item4: The World Food Programme supports with food and supplements in the early years.

2003.title: First Major International Donation
2003.item1: In May 2003, the first major international donation arrives (anonymous benefactor), a support that continues over time.

2009.title: Children's Playground
2009.item1: The children's playground is built together with the community.

2010.title: Technology and Entrepreneurship Training
2010.item1: The "Cigarrita" computer room is remodeled and equipped (donation from IRT, United Kingdom).
2010.item2: A bakery space is rented, with baking and pastry courses (mediated by FUMDIR).

2011.title: Casa Na
2011.item2: The bakery and a nursing station are permanently installed there.
2011.item3: In the previously rented space, a community clothing closet opens (alternative income source).

2013.title: The Malala Room is Born
2013.item1: The Malala Room is created, a space for teenagers (12+ years) during alternate hours.

2014.title: Senior Citizens and Community Works
2014.item1: A senior citizens group is incorporated, strengthening intergenerational exchange.
2014.item2: With the community and Fundación Techo, the main access road to the Foundation is paved.

2015.title: New Dining Hall and Infrastructure
2015.item1: A new dining hall is inaugurated, plus two classrooms, bathrooms, and an administrative office.

2017-2019.title: New Building for Clothing Closet and Music
2017-2019.item1: A three-story building is constructed for the clothing closet and music rooms.
2017-2019.item2: The vision of promoting the music program and a future orchestra is strengthened.

2019.title: Greta Thunberg Building
2019.item1: Recycled containers are acquired and the Greta Thunberg Building (2 floors) is constructed.
2019.item2: The San Jorge Room and the gymnastics room operate there.

2020.title: Racamandaca Mascot
2020.item1: The Foundation adopts its mascot Racamandaca, inspired by "las cigarritas".

2022.title: House Competition and Musical Momentum
2022.item1: The house competition begins (Jaguar, Shark, Caiman, and Condor), inspired by the English model.
2022.item2: The orchestra dream is strengthened thanks to a donation from a major Colombian NGO.
2022.item3: The Foundation's anthem is recorded (December 2022, with Claraluna and music teachers).

2023.title: First Orchestra Concert
2023.item1: The first orchestra concert takes place (June 2023), an institutional milestone.

2025.title: 24 Years of Impact
2025.item1: The Foundation reports over 24 years of service.
2025.item2: It has benefited 1,090 families and 1,877 children and adolescents.
2025.item3: It is consolidated as a benchmark for comprehensive care, education, art, sports, technology, and psychosocial support.

2026.title: San Jorge Room Expansion
2026.item1: The expansion of the San Jorge Room capacity is projected, creating the San Jorgito Room for children ages 6 to 12.

planPadrino.heroDescription: For just $25/month, give a child in Bogotá education, daily meals, and the chance to dream big. 100% tax-deductible. Cancel anytime.
planPadrino.howItWorksSubtitle: It takes less than 5 minutes to change a child's life.
planPadrino.chooseLevelSubtitle: Every plan includes education, daily meals, and personal progress reports. Cancel or change anytime.
planPadrino.whatIncludesSubtitle: Your sponsorship covers everything a child needs to learn, grow, and thrive.
planPadrino.testimonialQuote: Thanks to the Sponsorship Plan, my son now dreams of becoming an engineer. The foundation gave him the tools and love he needed to believe in himself.
planPadrino.testimonialRole: Mother of sponsored child, Ciudad Bolívar
planPadrino.faqSubtitle: Everything U.S. donors ask before sponsoring.
planPadrino.changeLifeDescription: Right now, a child in Bogotá is waiting for someone like you. Your tax-deductible sponsorship gives them education, daily meals, and a real chance at a better future. Cancel anytime.

education.title: Educational program
education.description: Academic reinforcement, workshops, and comprehensive pedagogical support for their education.

nutrition.title: Daily nutritious meals
nutrition.description: Balanced and nutritious food every day for $2.90 USD, ensuring their well-being.

reports.title: Personalized progress reports
reports.description: Detailed reports on the child's academic progress and comprehensive development.

certificate.title: Annual impact certificate
certificate.description: Official document certifying your contribution and the impact generated in the child's life.

photos.title: Photos and updates
photos.description: Images and news from your sponsored child's daily life at the foundation.

letter.title: Personalized letter from the child
letter.description: A letter written by your sponsored child sharing their dreams and gratitude.

taxDeductible.a: Yes — the Cigarra Foundation is an IRS-recognized 501(c)(3) nonprofit (EIN: 68-0505337). Your sponsorship is fully tax-deductible. You'll receive a tax receipt by email for your IRS filing.

cancel.a: Yes, no questions asked. Cancel or change your plan anytime by email or WhatsApp. No penalties, no commitments. If you cancel, the child continues to be served through other funding.

graduation.q: What if my child graduates or leaves?
graduation.a: We'll let you know and match you with a new child so your impact continues. You can also redirect your contribution to a different program if you prefer.

payment.q: How do I pay from the U.S.?
payment.a: Securely through PayPal — use any credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover), debit card, or PayPal balance. All amounts are in USD. Set up automatic monthly payments in under 2 minutes.

visit.q: Can I visit and meet my sponsored child?
visit.a: Absolutely! We host annual events for sponsors, and you can arrange a visit on any weekday. Many U.S. sponsors visit during trips to Colombia. We'll plan everything for you.

percentage.q: Does 100% of my money really go to the kids?
percentage.a: Yes — 100% goes directly to programs. Administrative costs are fully covered by corporate partners. We publish annual audited financial reports so you can see exactly where every dollar goes.

howToHelp.impactSubtitle: Pick an amount and see the real impact on a child's life.
howToHelp.customImpact: ${amount} supports education, meals, and programs for children in Bogotá

impactOptions.8: nutritious snacks for 4 children for a week
impactOptions.13: a full month of school supplies for 1 child
impactOptions.26: daily meals for 1 child for an entire month
impactOptions.51: a complete month of education + meals for 1 child

howToHelp.threeWaysSubtitle: All donations are 100% tax-deductible. Pay securely with PayPal or any major credit card.
howToHelp.donateDescription: One-time or monthly — 100% of your gift funds education, meals, and supplies. Tax receipt emailed instantly.
howToHelp.sponsorDescription: Sponsor a child in Ciudad Bolívar and transform their life with education, meals, and well-being. Your monthly commitment gives them the opportunity to dream big and grow in an environment full of possibilities for their integral development.
howToHelp.volunteerDescription: Help remotely from the U.S. — teach English, mentor kids, manage social media, or fundraise.
howToHelp.changeChildDescription: Your sponsorship covers a child's education and daily meals for the entire year. You'll receive personalized progress reports with photos and letters — so you can see the impact of your generosity firsthand. 100% tax-deductible.
howToHelp.yearsTransforming: years transforming lives in Ciudad Bolívar
howToHelp.plansFromMonthly: Flexible plans from $25/month to annual. See all options.
howToHelp.chooseImpactSubtitle: Every plan includes education, meals, and personalized reports. Choose what works for you.
howToHelp.realCostMeal: That's all it costs to feed a child a complete, nutritious meal
howToHelp.thisQuarter: this quarter across our nutrition programs
howToHelp.feedChildDaily: Just $2.90/day feeds a hungry child — that's less than a cup of coffee
howToHelp.volunteerWhatsAppDescription: Message us on WhatsApp — we'll match you with the right opportunity
howToHelp.faqSubtitle: Common questions from U.S. donors — answered.
howToHelp.finalCtaTitle: A child is counting on you
howToHelp.finalCtaDescription: Right now, a child in Bogotá needs your help. Your 100% tax-deductible gift today gives them education, meals, and hope for a better future.

howDonate.q: How do I donate from the U.S.?
howDonate.a: It's easy — click 'Give Now' and pay securely with PayPal or any major credit/debit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover). All transactions are in U.S. dollars. You can make a one-time gift or set up automatic monthly donations.

taxDeductible.a: Yes! The Cigarra Foundation is an IRS-recognized 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit (EIN: 68-0505337). Your donation is fully tax-deductible. You'll receive a tax receipt by email within 24 hours — keep it for your IRS filing.

transparency.q: How do I know my money actually reaches the children?
transparency.a: 100% of your donation goes directly to programs — $0 goes to admin overhead (administrative costs are covered by corporate partners). We publish annual audited financial reports. If you sponsor a child, you'll receive personal progress reports with photos showing exactly how your gift helps.

canVolunteer.q: Can I help from the U.S. without donating money?
canVolunteer.a: Absolutely! We need remote volunteers for English tutoring (via Zoom), social media management, fundraising, translation, and mentoring. No travel required. You can also visit us in Bogotá if you'd like. Email or WhatsApp us to get started.

whatIsPlanPadrino.a: For $25/month, you're paired with a specific child whose education, daily meals, and access to all 13 programs you fund. You'll receive their photo, progress reports, and personal letters throughout the year. You can cancel anytime — no commitments.

isLegit.q: How can I verify the Cigarra Foundation is a real nonprofit?
isLegit.a: We're fully transparent. You can verify our 501(c)(3) status on the IRS Tax Exempt Organization Search (EIN: 68-0505337) at irs.gov, or view our full financial history on ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer. We've been serving children in Bogotá since 2002.

andrea.name: Andrea Morales
andrea.quote: Volunteering at the Cigarra Foundation has been the most enriching experience of my life. Watching children advance academically and gain confidence in themselves is priceless.

carlos.name: Carlos Gutiérrez
carlos.quote: I share my love for music with the children of Ciudad Bolívar every Saturday. The joy on their faces when they manage to play a complete song is the best reward I could receive.

laura.name: Laura Jiménez
laura.quote: From the communications team, I help more people learn about the foundation's work. Every story we tell inspires someone new to join this cause.

volunteer.processSubtitle: In 3 simple steps, you can join our team of volunteers.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.description: Write to us on WhatsApp or email to tell us about your interest and availability. We'll respond within 24 hours.

start.title: Orientation and start

volunteer.ctaTitle: Your time can change a child's life
volunteer.ctaDescription: Whether you volunteer for an hour a week or an hour a month — every moment makes a difference. Join 50+ volunteers making an impact.

annual.name: Annual Plan

padrinoSection.taxDescription: As a 501(c)(3) organization, corporate donations to the Cigarra Foundation are deductible up to 10% of your taxable income under IRS Section 170(b)(2). At the 21% corporate tax rate, every dollar donated saves your company 21 cents in federal taxes.
padrinoSection.taxBenefit1: Tax deduction up to 10% of taxable income on your federal return (IRC §170(b)(2)). Excess carries forward 5 years.
padrinoSection.taxBenefit2: Tax receipt: The Cigarra Foundation provides an official donation receipt for your tax filing, delivered promptly by email.
padrinoSection.taxBenefit3: Corporate donors may also qualify for state-level tax benefits depending on your state of incorporation.
padrinoSection.includesTitle: What does the Sponsorship Plan include?

corporate.esalDescription: The Cigarra Foundation is a U.S.-registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization recognized by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). This means all donations from U.S. individuals and corporations are fully tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law. We are also registered as a nonprofit (ESAL) in Colombia.
corporate.esalLegalDescription: As a 501(c)(3) organization, the Cigarra Foundation meets all IRS requirements for tax-exempt status. Donors can deduct their contributions on their federal tax returns under IRS Section 170. We provide official tax receipts for all donations. Our EIN (Employer Identification Number) is 68-0505337.

taxCalc.description: See how much your company saves on taxes when donating to a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Corporate donations are deductible up to 10% of taxable income.
taxCalc.limitNote: Corporate deductions are limited to 10% of taxable income (IRC §170(b)(2)). Excess can be carried forward for 5 years.
taxCalc.limitWarning: Your donation exceeds 10% of taxable income. Only {limit} is deductible this year. The remaining {excess} can be carried forward up to 5 years.
taxCalc.disclaimer: This is an estimate only. Consult a CPA or tax advisor for your specific situation.

microsoft.quote: Technology for social good. We are proud to support an organization that brings digital literacy and STEM education to underserved communities.
microsoft.author: Microsoft Colombia

taxDeduction.question: How much can my company deduct?
taxDeduction.answer: U.S. corporations can deduct charitable contributions up to 10% of taxable income (IRC §170(b)(2)). At the 21% corporate tax rate, a $10,000 donation saves $2,100 in federal taxes. Excess amounts carry forward for 5 years.

certificate.question: Do I receive a tax receipt?
certificate.answer: Yes. We issue an official 501(c)(3) donation receipt via email within 48 hours of your contribution. This receipt is valid for your federal tax filing.

minimumDonation.question: Is there a minimum donation amount?
minimumDonation.answer: No minimum. We welcome contributions of any size. For corporate partnerships, most companies start with $1,000-$5,000 annually, but any amount makes a difference.

howFundsUsed.question: How are funds used?
howFundsUsed.answer: 100% of donations go directly to our programs: education, daily meals (breakfast, lunch, snack), music, arts, technology, sports, and psychological support for 192 children in Ciudad Bolivar.

reporting.question: What kind of reporting do I receive?
reporting.answer: Corporate partners receive quarterly impact reports with number of beneficiaries, program outcomes, photos, and stories. Annual donors also receive a personalized impact letter and event invitation.

verify.question: How can I verify your nonprofit status?
verify.answer: Search our EIN 68-0505337 on the IRS Tax Exempt Organization Search or ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer. We are a registered 501(c)(3) and ESAL in Colombia (NIT: 830.114.318-9).

privacy.seoDescription: Privacy and personal data treatment policy of the Cigarra Foundation in accordance with Colombian Law 1581 of 2012 and applicable regulations.
privacy.contactAddress: Calle 71Q sur No. 27-60, Barrio Puertas del Paraíso, Bogotá, Colombia
privacy.s1Content:
This policy is based on the following regulations:

• Colombian Constitution (Art. 15 — Right to privacy and habeas data)
• Law 1266 of 2008 — Financial habeas data
• Decision C-748 of 2011 — Constitutional review
• Law 1581 of 2012 — General personal data protection regime
• Decree 1377 of 2013 — Partial regulation of Law 1581
• Decree 886 of 2014 — National Database Registry
• Decree 1074 of 2015 — Single Regulatory Decree for the Commerce Sector
privacy.s2Content:
The Cigarra Foundation applies the following principles in the processing of personal data:

Legality: Processing activities are subject to the provisions of the law.

Purpose: Processing serves legitimate purposes previously communicated to the data subject.

Freedom: Requires prior, express, and informed consent from the data subject. Obtaining data without authorization is prohibited, except for legal exceptions.

Truthfulness: Information must be truthful, complete, accurate, up-to-date, verifiable, and understandable.

Transparency: The data subject is guaranteed access to information concerning them, without restrictions.

Restricted access: Only authorized persons may access personal data. Personal information will not be published on the internet without adequate technical controls.

Security: Technical, human, and administrative measures are implemented to protect data against alteration, loss, consultation, unauthorized use, or access.

Confidentiality: All persons involved in data processing are required to maintain confidentiality, even after the end of their employment or contractual relationship.
privacy.s3Content:
The Foundation manages identification, contact, personal, academic, and health data. Processing of sensitive data includes medical information for emergencies and psychological counseling, as well as biometric data (measurements, photographs, videos, recordings). Authorization for sensitive data processing is optional.

Main purposes of data processing:

• Providing early childhood care services
• Offering after-school programs for children ages 5 to 18
• Recreation services for senior citizens
• Educational management during enrollment at the foundation
• Recreational, sports, cultural, and educational activities
• Content generation and communications materials
• Complementary services (nutrition, psychology, physical activity)
• Transfer to oversight entities (Mayor's Office, Hospital Vista Hermosa, ICBF, district secretariats)
• Evaluation and implementation of health programs
• Ongoing communication with families regarding holistic development
• Compliance with legal information retention requirements
• Evaluation of financial suitability of suppliers
• Operational monitoring and physical security
• Service quality evaluation
• Statistical and historical processes
• Fundraising campaigns
privacy.s4Content:
Personal data subjects have the right to:

a) Know, update, rectify, or delete their personal data held by the Cigarra Foundation, including partial, inaccurate, incomplete data, or data subject to improper processing.

b) Request proof of the authorization granted for data processing.

c) Be informed about the use given to their personal data.

d) File complaints with the Superintendence of Industry and Commerce for violations of Law 1581 of 2012, after first filing an internal claim with the Foundation.

e) Revoke authorization when processing violates constitutional or legal principles.

f) Access personal data at no cost through the channels provided by the Foundation.
privacy.s5Content:
As data controller, the Foundation must:

• Guarantee the effective exercise of the habeas data right
• Request and maintain a copy of the authorization granted by the data subject
• Inform the data subject about the purposes of processing and their rights
• Maintain information under adequate security conditions to prevent alteration, loss, consultation, unauthorized use, or access
• Provide truthful and complete information to data processors
• Update information promptly
• Rectify incorrect data
• Provide only data whose processing has been authorized
• Respect confidentiality and require it from processors
• Promptly handle inquiries and claims
• Inform processors about pending claims
• Report security breaches to the competent authority
• Comply with instructions from the Superintendence of Industry and Commerce
privacy.s6Content:
Prior and informed authorization from the data subject is required, obtained through any verifiable means or technological mechanism that allows unequivocal expression of consent.

Authorization is not required when:

• A public entity requires it in the exercise of legal functions or by court order
• The data is public in nature
• There is a medical or health emergency
• Processing is authorized by law for historical, statistical, or scientific purposes
• The data pertains to Civil Registry records

For sensitive data, it will be clearly indicated which data is sensitive, its purpose, and that there is no obligation to authorize its processing.
privacy.s7Content:
When this policy cannot be delivered directly to the data subject, the Foundation will inform through a privacy notice about its existence and how to access it, prior to and in any case no later than the time of collection of personal data.

The complete policy is published at www.cigarra.org.
privacy.s8Content:
The following may exercise the rights provided in this policy:

• The data subject, upon verification of their identity
• Successors in case of death or incapacity of the data subject
• The duly accredited representative or attorney of the data subject
• By stipulation or mandate in favor of another
• For minors, their parents or legal representatives
privacy.s9Title: Person Responsible for Inquiries and Claims
privacy.s9Content:
The Data Protection Officer (Foundation Administrator) is responsible for handling inquiries and claims, defining and implementing actions in accordance with current regulations.

Contact: esperanza.duque@cigarra.org
privacy.s10Content: Inquiries will be addressed within a maximum period of ten (10) business days from the date of receipt. When it is not possible to address the inquiry within said period, the interested party will be informed of the reasons for the delay and the date on which it will be addressed, which in no case may exceed five (5) business days following the expiration of the initial period.
privacy.s11Content:
Claims for correction, updating, deletion of data, or for alleged failure to comply with legal duties will follow this procedure:

1. The data subject must file a request identifying the description of facts, their address, and the documents they wish to submit. If the request is incomplete, the interested party will be required to complete it within five (5) days. If two (2) months pass from the completion request without the interested party providing the required information, it will be understood that they have withdrawn the claim.

2. Upon receipt of the complete claim, the legend "claim in process" and the reason for it will be included in the database, within a maximum of two (2) business days. This legend will be maintained until the claim is resolved.

3. The maximum period for resolving the claim is fifteen (15) business days from the day following receipt of the complete claim. If it is not possible to address it within said period, the interested party will be informed of the reasons for the delay and the new resolution date, which in no case may exceed eight (8) business days following the expiration of the initial period.
privacy.s12Content: The Cigarra Foundation reserves the right to modify this policy at any time. Any substantial changes will be promptly communicated to data subjects through the usual contact channels or through the institutional website.
privacy.s13Content:
Personal data will be retained in accordance with the principles of necessity, reasonableness, expiration, and temporality:

• Minor beneficiaries: During the term of the relationship and compliance with legal or contractual obligations.
• Former beneficiaries: Up to ten (10) years maximum after the end of the relationship.
• Employees and suppliers: During the term of the contractual relationship and in accordance with applicable legal periods.
• Program or employment candidates: One (1) year after the end of the process.

Authorization remains in effect as long as the obligations that motivated it subsist. Once legal terms are fulfilled and the relationships that gave rise to processing are extinguished, data will be deleted.

This policy is effective as of January 1, 2018.

cookiePolicy.seoDescription: Information about the use of cookies on the Cigarra Foundation website, types of cookies used, and how to manage them.
cookiePolicy.heroSubtitle: Transparent information about how we use cookies to improve your experience on our website.
cookiePolicy.s1Content:
Cookies are small text files that websites store on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile phone) when you visit them. They are widely used to make websites work more efficiently, provide information to site owners, and enhance the user experience.

Cookies can be "first-party" (set by the website you are visiting) or "third-party" (set by an external service that appears on the page).
cookiePolicy.s2Content:
Strictly necessary: Essential for the basic functioning of the website. These include session cookies, language preference, and the record of your cookie decision. They cannot be disabled as the site would not function properly without them. They do not store personally identifiable information.

Performance and analytics: Help us understand how visitors interact with our site through anonymous data collection. We use Google Analytics with IP anonymization enabled, which means your IP address is shortened before being processed. These cookies are only activated if you give your explicit consent.

Targeting and advertising: We currently do not use advertising or tracking cookies for targeting purposes. If we implement such cookies in the future, we will update this policy and request your prior consent.

Functionality: Remember your preferences such as selected language and your cookie usage decision, allowing a more personalized experience on future visits.
cookiePolicy.s3Content:
Google Analytics (provider: Google LLC): We use Google Analytics to analyze website usage. Google Analytics generates statistical information about visits through cookies stored on your device. The generated information is used to create reports about site usage.

Google stores this information in accordance with its privacy policy. You can review Google's privacy policy at: https://policies.google.com/privacy

We have configured Google Analytics with the following protection measures:

• IP anonymization (anonymize_ip: true) — your IP address is shortened before processing
• Data is not shared with Google for advertising purposes
• Google Analytics cookies are only loaded after obtaining your explicit consent in the "Performance" category
cookiePolicy.s4Content:
The cookies we use have different retention periods:

Session cookies: Automatically deleted when you close your browser.

Persistent cookies:
• Cookie consent preference (cigarra_cookie_consent): Stored in localStorage with no expiration date until the user manually deletes it or clears browser data.
• Cookie preferences (cigarra_cookie_prefs): Same duration as above.
• Google Analytics cookies (_ga): 2 years.
• Google Analytics cookies (_ga_*): 2 years.
cookiePolicy.s5Content:
You have several options for controlling cookies:

From our site: You can modify your preferences at any time by clicking the "Cookie Preferences" link in the footer of our website. This will reopen the cookie settings panel.

From your browser: You can configure your browser to reject all cookies, accept only some, or notify you when a site tries to set a cookie.

• Google Chrome: Settings > Privacy and Security > Cookies and other site data
• Mozilla Firefox: Options > Privacy & Security > Cookies and Site Data
• Safari: Preferences > Privacy > Cookies and website data
• Microsoft Edge: Settings > Cookies and site permissions

Please note that if you disable all cookies, some website features may not be available.
cookiePolicy.s6Content:
In accordance with applicable regulations (Colombian Law 1581 of 2012, the EU General Data Protection Regulation, and the ePrivacy Directive), non-essential cookies are only installed on your device after you give your explicit consent through our cookie banner.

Strictly necessary cookies do not require consent, as they are essential for the site to function.

You can withdraw your consent at any time through the "Cookie Preferences" link located in the footer. Upon withdrawing consent, non-essential cookies will stop loading and existing ones will be deleted according to their retention policy.
cookiePolicy.s7Content:
The Cigarra Foundation reserves the right to update this Cookie Policy at any time. Any significant changes will be communicated through a visible notice on our website.

We recommend periodically reviewing this page to stay informed about our use of cookies.
cookiePolicy.s8Content:
If you have questions, comments, or requests related to our Cookie Policy or the processing of your personal data, you can contact us through the following channels:

Email: info@cigarra.org
Phone: +57 321 246 5421
Address: Calle 71Q sur No. 27-60, Bogotá, Colombia

You can also review our Personal Data Treatment Policy for complete information about how we protect your data.
